# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

The following versions are currently supported with security updates.

| Version | Supported |
|---------|-----------|
| Latest Release | ✅ |
| Development Branch (`main`) | ✅ |
| Older Releases | ❌ |

---

## Reporting a Vulnerability

If you discover a security vulnerability, **please do not create a public GitHub Issue.**

Instead, report it privately by contacting the maintainers.

When submitting a report, please include:

- A detailed description of the vulnerability
- Steps to reproduce the issue
- The affected version or commit hash
- Proof of Concept (if applicable)
- Potential impact
- Any suggested mitigations

We will:

1. Acknowledge your report within **72 hours**.
2. Investigate and validate the issue.
3. Work on a fix as quickly as possible.
4. Coordinate responsible disclosure with the reporter.
5. Credit the reporter (if desired) after the issue has been resolved.

---

## Responsible Disclosure

Please give us reasonable time to investigate and fix reported vulnerabilities before publicly disclosing them.

Public disclosure before a patch is available may put users at unnecessary risk.

---

## Scope

This policy applies to:

- Source code
- GitHub Actions workflows
- Documentation
- Configuration files
- APIs
- Dependencies maintained by this project

Third-party software and dependencies should be reported to their respective maintainers unless the vulnerability is caused by this project.

---

## Security Best Practices

If you contribute to this project, please:

- Keep dependencies up to date.
- Never commit secrets, API keys, or credentials.
- Enable MFA on your GitHub account.
- Review pull requests before merging.
- Follow secure coding practices.
- Report suspicious behavior immediately.

---

## Security Updates

Security fixes will be released as soon as practical.

Critical vulnerabilities may result in immediate patches and releases outside of the normal development cycle.

---

## Hall of Fame

We appreciate responsible security researchers who help improve this project.

Contributors who responsibly disclose valid vulnerabilities may be acknowledged here (with permission).

- *No reports yet.*

---

Thank you for helping keep this project secure.
